testTitle: IELTS Level 4.6 Advanced Finance & Banking Test - Varied Answers

sections.title: Listening
sections.description: 40 questions • Approximately 30 minutes • You will hear each recording only once

parts.title: Part 1: Discussion about financial technology and digital banking
parts.instructions: Listen to the conversation and answer questions 1-10.

script.narrator:
Good morning. This is the International English Language Testing System, IELTS. You will hear a number of different recordings and you will have to answer questions on what you hear. There will be time for you to read the instructions and questions and you will have a chance to check your work. All the recordings will be played once only. The test is in four parts. At the end of the test you will be given ten minutes to transfer your answers to an answer sheet.

Now turn to Part 1. You will hear a conversation between a fintech analyst and a banking executive about digital transformation in finance. Look at questions 1 to 10. You now have some time to look at the questions.

[Pause]

Now listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 10.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: The traditional banking model has demonstrated significant limitations in meeting evolving customer expectations. Its emphasis on physical branches and standardized products failed to account for digital convenience, personalized services, and real-time transaction processing.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: Indeed, the digital banking revolution acknowledges these shortcomings but still operates within a fundamentally regulated framework. What's needed is a more innovative approach that recognizes the role of blockchain technology, artificial intelligence, and seamless customer experiences.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Precisely. The success of digital-only banks demonstrates the importance of mobile-first design combined with data-driven personalization. However, simply replicating these models is insufficient—each market requires context-specific strategies based on its unique regulatory environment and customer behavior.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: The concept of 'open banking' developed in European markets provides valuable insights here. Financial services evolve by integrating third-party providers that enhance existing banking capabilities. This interoperability creates both opportunities and challenges for traditional institutions.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Excellent point. This underscores the importance of strategic partnerships rather than isolated innovation. The challenge lies in maintaining security standards while enabling data sharing through APIs and building complementary capabilities in cybersecurity and user experience.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: The regulatory transformation adds another layer of complexity. Banks face the dual challenge of complying with traditional regulations while adapting to new digital frameworks. This requires investments in both compliance systems and innovation labs.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Moreover, the cybersecurity imperative necessitates robust protection measures. The traditional approach of 'security as an afterthought' is no longer viable. Financial institutions must embed security considerations from the initial design phase.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: This implies rethinking customer relationships as well. Rather than offering one-size-fits-all products, banks should use data analytics to provide personalized financial advice and proactive service recommendations.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: The role of human expertise remains crucial but must evolve. Instead of routine transactions, bank staff should focus on complex advisory services, relationship management, and financial education. This requires reskilling programs and cultural transformation.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: Ultimately, successful digital transformation must balance technological innovation with customer trust, operational efficiency with regulatory compliance, and short-term profitability with long-term strategic positioning.

questions.questionText: What has demonstrated limitations according to the speakers?
questions.translation: Điều gì đã thể hiện những hạn chế theo các diễn giả?

questions.questionText: What failed to account for digital convenience?
questions.translation: Điều gì đã không tính đến sự tiện lợi kỹ thuật số?

questions.questionText: What does digital banking still operate within?
questions.translation: Ngân hàng số vẫn hoạt động trong khuôn khổ nào?

questions.questionText: What demonstrates the importance of mobile-first design?
questions.translation: Điều gì chứng minh tầm quan trọng của thiết kế ưu tiên di động?

questions.questionText: What concept provides insights about financial evolution?
questions.translation: Khái niệm nào cung cấp hiểu biết về tiến hóa tài chính?

questions.questionText: What creates both opportunities and challenges?
questions.translation: Điều gì tạo ra cả cơ hội và thách thức?

questions.questionText: What dual challenge do banks face?
questions.translation: Ngân hàng phải đối mặt với thách thức kép nào?

questions.questionText: What approach is no longer viable?
questions.translation: Phương pháp nào không còn khả thi?

questions.questionText: What should bank staff focus on instead of routine transactions?
questions.translation: Nhân viên ngân hàng nên tập trung vào điều gì thay vì giao dịch thường lệ?

questions.questionText: What must digital transformation balance?
questions.translation: Chuyển đổi số phải cân bằng điều gì?

parts.title: Part 2: Lecture on investment strategies and portfolio management
parts.instructions: Listen to the lecture and answer questions 11-20.

script.narrator:
Now turn to Part 2. You will hear a lecture on investment strategies and portfolio management. Look at questions 11 to 20. You now have some time to look at the questions.

[Pause]

Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 20.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: The evolution of investment management represents one of the most significant transformations in modern finance, creating new portfolio strategies that challenge conventional financial theory. Modern investment approaches exhibit distinctive characteristics including factor-based investing, ESG integration, and quantitative analysis. These features create sophisticated risk-return dynamics that can lead to enhanced portfolio performance. The science of portfolio management fundamentally differs from traditional stock picking—value is created through systematic diversification rather than individual security selection. This creates novel regulatory challenges, as investment product classification becomes more complex and risk management operates across multiple asset classes. Data analytics and machine learning enable sophisticated risk modeling and personalized investment solutions, raising concerns about algorithmic bias and systemic risk. The regulatory framework for modern investment strategies remains underdeveloped, struggling to balance innovation promotion with investor protection. Traditional asset class boundaries are increasingly blurred in multi-asset portfolios, requiring integrated risk management approaches. The future evolution of investment management will likely involve greater attention to sustainable investing, digital assets, and behavioral finance as investors grapple with the implications of these sophisticated new investment approaches.

questions.questionText: What represents significant transformation in finance?
questions.translation: Điều gì đại diện cho sự chuyển đổi quan trọng trong tài chính?

questions.questionText: What distinctive characteristics do modern investments exhibit?
questions.translation: Đầu tư hiện đại thể hiện những đặc điểm khác biệt nào?

questions.questionText: What dynamics can lead to enhanced performance?
questions.translation: Động lực nào có thể dẫn đến hiệu suất được cải thiện?

questions.questionText: How does portfolio value creation differ?
questions.translation: Tạo giá trị danh mục khác biệt như thế nào?

questions.questionText: What becomes more complex in modern investing?
questions.translation: Điều gì trở nên phức tạp hơn trong đầu tư hiện đại?

questions.questionText: What enables sophisticated risk modeling?
questions.translation: Điều gì cho phép mô hình hóa rủi ro tinh vi?

questions.questionText: What remains underdeveloped?
questions.translation: Điều gì vẫn còn kém phát triển?

questions.questionText: What are increasingly blurred?
questions.translation: Điều gì ngày càng trở nên mờ nhạt?

questions.questionText: What requires integrated approaches?
questions.translation: Điều gì yêu cầu cách tiếp cận tích hợp?

questions.questionText: What will receive greater attention?
questions.translation: Điều gì sẽ nhận được sự quan tâm lớn hơn?

parts.title: Part 3: Discussion about risk management and financial regulation
parts.instructions: Listen to the discussion and answer questions 21-30.

script.narrator:
Now turn to Part 3. You will hear a discussion between a risk manager and a regulatory expert about financial risk management. Look at questions 21 to 30. You now have some time to look at the questions.

[Pause]

Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 30.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: The landscape of financial risk management presents fascinating complexities. Modern financial systems generate interconnected risks through global markets, derivative instruments, and digital platforms, yet they also create sophisticated hedging opportunities, diversification benefits, and risk transfer mechanisms. The fundamental challenge is maximizing risk-adjusted returns while minimizing systemic vulnerabilities.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: This is precisely where advanced analytics offers transformative potential. Stress testing, scenario analysis, and real-time monitoring can optimize risk exposure, reduce unexpected losses, and improve capital allocation. However, the risk management benefits must be balanced against compliance costs to avoid creating excessive regulatory burden.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: The capital adequacy mechanism is particularly important here. Regulatory investments in risk management create substantial stability benefits that are often underestimated. Better capital planning could enhance financial resilience while reducing bailout risks.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: Cybersecurity risk is becoming as crucial as traditional credit risk. Digital protection measures are now a prerequisite for financial operations. But we must ensure that security protocols don't create operational bottlenecks or customer inconvenience.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: The governance dimension is critical. Effective risk management requires integrated oversight across traditionally siloed departments. This demands new organizational capabilities and collaborative frameworks that engage all stakeholders as risk owners rather than passive observers.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: The implementation models for regulatory compliance also need rethinking. Principles-based regulation can leverage market discipline and innovation, but it must be structured to protect systemic stability and ensure consistent enforcement rather than creating regulatory arbitrage.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Financial innovation risk is another crucial consideration. Institutions must balance product innovation with risk control while maintaining the competitive edge that drives market leadership. This requires strategic risk appetite frameworks.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: The international coordination of financial regulation is equally important. Cross-border supervision, information sharing, and harmonized standards can make global finance more stable while creating level playing fields. The cost of regulatory fragmentation far exceeds the cost of coordinated oversight.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Finally, we must consider the behavioral dimension. Successful risk management can't exist as purely quantitative models divorced from human psychology. Behavioral economics and organizational culture are essential for effective risk governance.

conversation.speaker: male
conversation.text: Ultimately, modern risk management is about using data, technology, and governance principles to create financial systems that are not only profitable and innovative but also resilient, transparent, and trustworthy—systems that serve economic growth while protecting financial stability.

questions.questionText: What complexity does financial risk present?
questions.translation: Rủi ro tài chính thể hiện sự phức tạp gì?

questions.questionText: What do advanced analytics optimize?
questions.translation: Phân tích nâng cao tối ưu hóa điều gì?

questions.questionText: What mechanism is important for financial resilience?
questions.translation: Cơ chế nào quan trọng cho khả năng phục hồi tài chính?

questions.questionText: What is becoming as crucial as credit risk?
questions.translation: Điều gì đang trở nên quan trọng như rủi ro tín dụng?

questions.questionText: What does risk management require across departments?
questions.translation: Quản lý rủi ro yêu cầu điều gì giữa các phòng ban?

questions.questionText: What must principles-based regulation protect?
questions.translation: Quy định dựa trên nguyên tắc phải bảo vệ điều gì?

questions.questionText: What is crucial for financial innovation?
questions.translation: Điều gì quan trọng cho đổi mới tài chính?

questions.questionText: What can make global finance more stable?
questions.translation: Điều gì có thể làm cho tài chính toàn cầu ổn định hơn?

questions.questionText: What is essential for effective risk governance?
questions.translation: Điều gì cần thiết cho quản trị rủi ro hiệu quả?

questions.questionText: What should financial systems serve?
questions.translation: Hệ thống tài chính nên phục vụ điều gì?

parts.title: Part 4: Lecture on sustainable finance and ESG investing
parts.instructions: Listen to the lecture and answer questions 31-40.

script.narrator:
Now turn to Part 4. You will hear a lecture on sustainable finance and ESG investing. Look at questions 31 to 40. You now have some time to look at the questions.

[Pause]

Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Sustainable finance provides crucial insights into the integration of environmental, social, and governance factors into investment decision-making. The quality of ESG integration—defined as the systematic consideration of sustainability criteria in financial analysis—explains more of the variation in long-term investment outcomes than traditional financial metrics alone. Comprehensive ESG frameworks that assess climate risk, social impact, and governance quality enable resilient portfolios. Conversely, narrow financial analysis that ignores sustainability factors exposes investors to transition risks and reputational damage. The persistence of short-term investment horizons presents a central challenge: why do many investors still prioritize quarterly returns over sustainable value creation? Historical performance analysis emphasizes the role of regulatory changes and stakeholder pressure in driving ESG adoption. The alignment of financial incentives fundamentally influences sustainable investment flows, as asset managers respond to client demands for responsible investing. Building effective ESG integration requires not just data analysis but also cultural commitment to long-term thinking. The challenge of sustainable finance lies in changing not just investment processes but also performance measurement and compensation structures. Understanding these dynamics is essential for designing investment strategies that achieve both financial returns and positive societal impact.

questions.questionText: What provides insights into ESG integration?
questions.translation: Điều gì cung cấp hiểu biết về tích hợp ESG?

questions.questionText: What explains long-term outcomes better?
questions.translation: Điều gì giải thích kết quả dài hạn tốt hơn?

questions.questionText: What do comprehensive frameworks enable?
questions.translation: Khuôn khổ toàn diện cho phép điều gì?

questions.questionText: What does narrow analysis expose investors to?
questions.translation: Phân tích hẹp làm lộ diện nhà đầu tư với điều gì?

questions.questionText: What presents a central challenge?
questions.translation: Điều gì đặt ra một thách thức trung tâm?

questions.questionText: What emphasizes regulatory changes?
questions.translation: Điều gì nhấn mạnh thay đổi quy định?

questions.questionText: What fundamentally influences investment flows?
questions.translation: Điều gì ảnh hưởng cơ bản đến dòng đầu tư?

questions.questionText: What does ESG integration require?
questions.translation: Tích hợp ESG yêu cầu điều gì?

questions.questionText: What does sustainable finance require changing?
questions.translation: Tài chính bền vững yêu cầu thay đổi điều gì?

questions.questionText: What is essential for dual-objective strategies?
questions.translation: Điều gì cần thiết cho chiến lược mục tiêu kép?

passages.title: Passage 1: The Evolution of Central Banking and Monetary Policy
passages.content: The evolution of central banking represents a fundamental transformation in understanding the role of monetary authorities in economic stability. Modern central banking differs from traditional approaches in its emphasis on inflation targeting, financial stability, and unconventional policy tools. These developments create complex policy frameworks that must address economic cycles, financial markets, and global interdependencies. Forward guidance conceptualizes monetary policy as a communication strategy influencing market expectations—connected through credibility and transparency. The independence of central banks determines their ability to make unpopular but necessary decisions, highlighting the importance of institutional design and political insulation. The implementation of monetary policy exhibits sophisticated transmission mechanisms, with policy effects varying across economic sectors, explaining the importance of targeted interventions despite general tools. The digital transformation has created new monetary policy challenges related to digital currencies, payment systems, and financial innovation. Quantitative easing and negative interest rates challenge traditional monetary theory, emphasizing balance sheet management and policy innovation. The integration of macroprudential policy introduces novel considerations regarding financial stability, systemic risk, and the interaction between monetary and regulatory functions. Understanding modern central banking requires analyzing not only interest rate decisions but also communication strategies, financial stability measures, and international coordination through comprehensive policy frameworks.

questions.questionText: What represents fundamental transformation?
What characteristics create complex frameworks?
What conceptualizes policy as communication?
What determines decision-making ability?
What exhibits sophisticated transmission?
What has created new challenges?
What challenges traditional theory?
What introduces novel stability considerations?
What requires analyzing multiple dimensions?
What coordinates through comprehensive frameworks?

passages.title: Passage 2: Behavioral Finance and Market Psychology
passages.content: Behavioral finance integrates insights from psychology and neuroscience to enhance understanding of financial decision-making. Traditional finance often assumed rational, utility-maximizing investors with efficient markets, but behavioral finance reveals systematic cognitive biases and emotional influences that create market anomalies. Investor psychology itself creates market dynamics that deviate from fundamental values, creating behavioral patterns where herding, overconfidence, and loss aversion drive price movements. Present bias leads to short-term trading and speculation, while disposition effect causes investors to hold losing positions too long. Social influences including media sentiment and peer behavior impact market trends and bubble formations. Mental accounting—the psychological categorization of money—shapes investment behavior and can persist despite financial optimization principles, affecting portfolio construction, risk tolerance, and trading frequency. Nudge-based interventions have demonstrated success in improving retirement savings, investment diversification, and financial literacy by redesigning choice architectures. However, behavioral approaches face limitations in addressing market efficiency and institutional constraints. The integration of behavioral insights with traditional finance offers a more comprehensive understanding of market dynamics and more effective investment strategies. This requires context-specific applications and careful attention to ethical considerations regarding investor protection and market manipulation. Behavioral finance ultimately aims to create investment approaches that work with human psychology as it is, rather than as traditional models assume it to be.

questions.questionText: What integrates insights from psychology?
What reveals systematic cognitive biases?
What creates behavioral market patterns?
What leads to short-term trading?
What impacts market trends?
What shapes investment through categorization?
What have demonstrated success in improvement?
What do behavioral approaches face limitations in addressing?
What offers more comprehensive understanding?
What does behavioral finance aim to work with?
options: D] Human psychology as it is

passages.title: Passage 3: Corporate Finance and Strategic Financial Management
passages.content: Corporate finance examines the financial decisions of businesses and the creation of shareholder value, focusing on capital allocation, funding strategies, and financial risk management. Corporate financial decisions typically involve complex trade-offs between risk and return, with capital structure, dividend policy, and investment choices creating interconnected financial outcomes. This strategic integration explains the holistic nature of corporate financial management. Financial planning processes—where multiple stakeholders including management, investors, and creditors interact—can create both value creation opportunities and agency conflicts. Economic value principles and market conditions shape how companies allocate resources and structure financing. The timing and sequencing of financial decisions crucially influence corporate value, with major investments often coordinated with financing arrangements when market windows are favorable. Risk management strategies that identify and mitigate financial exposures can enhance corporate resilience, though they may involve hedging costs and complexity. The credibility of financial reporting affects corporate valuation, as investors may discount company shares if they question accounting quality. External factors including interest rates, economic cycles, and industry dynamics influence corporate financial decisions but cannot substitute for sound financial governance. Successful corporate financial strategies typically combine financial expertise with strategic vision, creating sustainable value while managing risks through prudent capital management, optimal financing mix, and strategic investments. Understanding corporate finance is essential for designing financial policies that achieve both operational excellence and shareholder value creation.

questions.questionText: What examines corporate financial decisions?
What problem explains holistic management?
What can create value and conflicts?
What shape resource allocation?
When are major investments coordinated?
What can enhance corporate resilience?
What affects corporate valuation?
What cannot substitute for sound governance?
What do successful strategies combine?
options: A) Basic skills and simple goals
B] Traditional knowledge and routine planning
C] Simple finance and general management
D] Financial expertise and strategic vision

questions.questionText: What is essential for designing financial policies?

tasks.title: Writing Task 1
tasks.instructions: You should spend about 20 minutes on this task. The diagram below shows the traditional banking model compared to the digital banking ecosystem. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant. Write at least 150 words.
tasks.description: You will be presented with a diagram showing banking models and asked to describe the information in your own words.

sampleData.title: Traditional vs Digital Banking Models

Traditional Banking: Physical branches → Paper processes → Limited hours → Standardized products
Digital Banking: Mobile apps → Automated processes → 24/7 access → Personalized services → API integrations

sampleData.description: Comparison of traditional branch-based banking with modern digital banking ecosystem

tasks.title: Writing Task 2
tasks.instructions: You should spend about 40 minutes on this task. Some argue that cryptocurrency and blockchain technology will disrupt traditional financial systems and increase financial inclusion, while others believe they pose significant risks to financial stability and enable illicit activities. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion. Write at least 250 words.
tasks.description: You will be presented with contrasting views on cryptocurrency impacts and asked to discuss them.
keyPoints: Arguments about financial disruption and inclusion
Arguments about stability risks and illicit activities

questions.questionText: _____ comprehensive the financial regulations, their effectiveness ultimately depends on enforcement capacity and market compliance.
questions.translation: Dù các quy định tài chính có toàn diện đến đâu, hiệu quả của chúng cuối cùng phụ thuộc vào năng lực thực thi và tuân thủ thị trường.

questions.questionText: The investment strategy, _____ on sophisticated risk modeling, demonstrated remarkable resilience during market volatility.
questions.explanation: Past participle in reduced relative clause
questions.translation: Chiến lược đầu tư, dựa trên mô hình hóa rủi ro tinh vi, đã thể hiện khả năng phục hồi đáng kể trong thời kỳ biến động thị trường.

questions.questionText: So profound _____ that traditional banking models struggle to adapt.
options: A) has the fintech disruption been
B) the fintech disruption has been
questions.translation: Sự gián đoạn fintech sâu sắc đến mức các mô hình ngân hàng truyền thống phải vật lộn để thích nghi.

questions.questionText: The regulatory committee recommended that cybersecurity measures _____ integrated into all financial operations.
questions.translation: Ủy ban quản lý khuyến nghị rằng các biện pháp an ninh mạng phải được tích hợp vào tất cả hoạt động tài chính.

questions.questionText: _____ the complex interdependencies of global markets, local economic policies can have international repercussions.
questions.translation: Xét đến sự phụ thuộc lẫn nhau phức tạp của thị trường toàn cầu, các chính sách kinh tế địa phương có thể có tác động quốc tế.

questions.questionText: The financial ecosystem, _____ numerous institutions and technologies, has become increasingly interconnected.
questions.explanation: Present participle in reduced relative clause
questions.translation: Hệ sinh thái tài chính, bao gồm nhiều tổ chức và công nghệ, đã trở nên ngày càng kết nối với nhau.

questions.questionText: So rapidly _____ that regulatory frameworks struggle to keep pace.
questions.translation: Đổi mới tài chính phát triển nhanh đến mức các khuôn khổ quy định phải vật lộn để theo kịp.

questions.questionText: The central bank insisted that inflation targets _____ consistently maintained.
questions.translation: Ngân hàng trung ương nhấn mạnh rằng mục tiêu lạm phát phải được duy trì nhất quán.

questions.questionText: _____ the prevailing market uncertainties, sustainable investments continue to demonstrate resilience.
questions.translation: Bất chấp những bất ổn thị trường hiện hành, đầu tư bền vững tiếp tục thể hiện khả năng phục hồi.

questions.questionText: The financial system, _____ over centuries of development, faces unprecedented digital challenges.
questions.explanation: Past participle in reduced relative clause
questions.translation: Hệ thống tài chính, được xây dựng qua nhiều thế kỷ phát triển, đối mặt với những thách thức kỹ thuật số chưa từng có.

questions.questionText: So significant _____ that it has transformed global capital flows.
options: A] has the rise of emerging markets been
B] the rise of emerging markets has been
C] was the rise of emerging markets
D] the rise of emerging markets was
questions.translation: Sự trỗi dậy của các thị trường mới nổi quan trọng đến mức nó đã biến đổi dòng vốn toàn cầu.

questions.questionText: The international agreement requires that transparency standards _____ uniformly applied.
questions.translation: Thỏa thuận quốc tế yêu cầu rằng các tiêu chuẩn minh bạch phải được áp dụng thống nhất.

questions.questionText: _____ the sophisticated risk models, predicting market behavior remains challenging.
questions.translation: Bất chấp các mô hình rủi ro tinh vi, dự đoán hành vi thị trường vẫn đầy thách thức.

questions.questionText: The regulatory framework, _____ to address systemic risks, requires continuous updating.
questions.explanation: Past participle in reduced relative clause
questions.translation: Khuôn khổ quy định, được thiết kế để giải quyết rủi ro hệ thống, yêu cầu cập nhật liên tục.

questions.questionText: So complex _____ that traditional financial analysis has proven inadequate.
questions.translation: Các thị trường hiện đại trở nên phức tạp đến mức phân tích tài chính truyền thống đã chứng minh là không đầy đủ.

questions.questionText: The financial reform requires that governance standards _____ consistently enforced.
questions.translation: Cải cách tài chính yêu cầu rằng các tiêu chuẩn quản trị phải được thực thi nhất quán.

questions.questionText: _____ the detailed market analysis, investment decisions involve uncertainty.
questions.translation: Bất chấp phân tích thị trường chi tiết, quyết định đầu tư vẫn liên quan đến sự không chắc chắn.

questions.questionText: The banking model, _____ on customer-centric principles, has achieved remarkable adoption.
questions.explanation: Past participle in reduced relative clause
questions.translation: Mô hình ngân hàng, dựa trên các nguyên tắc lấy khách hàng làm trung tâm, đã đạt được mức độ chấp nhận đáng kể.

questions.questionText: So rapidly _____ that traditional institutions have struggled to adapt.
options: A] is the payment landscape changing
B] the payment landscape is changing
C] has the payment landscape changed
D] the payment landscape has changed
questions.translation: Bối cảnh thanh toán thay đổi nhanh đến mức các tổ chức truyền thống phải vật lộn để thích nghi.

questions.questionText: The financial policy mandates that stability measures _____ prioritized during crises.
questions.translation: Chính sách tài chính yêu cầu rằng các biện pháp ổn định phải được ưu tiên trong khủng hoảng.

metadata.author: IELTS Practice System
metadata.notes: IELTS Level 4.6 advanced finance test with properly varied correct answers. Correct answers are distributed across A, B, C, D to prevent guessing patterns while maintaining consistent scoring.
